title: Part 2: Largest Rectangle in a Histogram

cues.label: Increasing stack
cues.headline: Bars kept short→tall
cues.narration: Keep a stack of bar indices with increasing heights; each still might extend right.

cues.label: A wall arrives
cues.headline: Shorter bar triggers a pop
cues.narration: When a new bar is shorter than the top, the top can extend no further right, so measure it now.

cues.label: Height
cues.headline: The popped bar's height
cues.narration: The rectangle's height is the popped bar's own height.

cues.label: Width off the stack
cues.headline: Left neighbor + current index
cues.narration: The exposed neighbor is the nearest shorter bar left; the current index is the wall right; width = i - newTop - 1.

cues.label: Track the max
cues.headline: Height × width
cues.narration: Multiply height by width and keep the running maximum; here 5×2 = 10 wins.

cues.label: Sentinel
cues.headline: Flush the leftovers
cues.narration: A trailing zero-height bar pops everything remaining through the same loop — no cleanup pass.
